corrected_bucket_evidence: from the post-default audit (rendered kernels): genuine norm/rope/residual + small reduces ~2.3ms GPU-busy, FFN activation (silu/gate, unfused) ~1.5ms GPU-busy. llama fuses these (rmsnorm 613 + rope 365 + silu 129 + residual 84 us vs tinygrad's many unfused kernels).
caveat: tinygrad GPU-busy sum (13.7ms) >> wall (11.7ms): the small-ops are HEAVILY OVERLAPPED, so the WALL transfer of any fusion is UNCERTAIN (unlike the KV materialization, which the MAXC-shrink proved is on the wall critical path).
first_bounded_gate: prove ONE fusion (e.g. silu(gate)*up into the FFN-GEMV epilogue, or RMSNorm+residual) removes a measured kernel group AND clears >=1-2% W==D, BEFORE any broad codegen work
note: Justified as the fallback lane (runtime-KV is core-blocked), but lower confidence than runtime-KV's impact because of overlap. Scope written: docs/small-ops-activation-fusion-scope-20260623.md
